anti_patterns_to_avoid: Moving state without proving ownership or invalidation boundaries
Hiding work behind Suspense or debounce while INP remains slow
reasoning: perf-151 treats Avoiding render in scroll handler as a react_rendering bottleneck. The fix is valid only when React Profiler, browser INP trace, render-count assertions, and interaction replay show render_count_per_action (count) meeting the row target: only components with changed inputs re-render; correctness output must remain unchanged and the rollback path must be explicit.

after_metrics: perf-151 optimized result for Avoiding render in scroll handler: render_count_per_action must meet target (only components with changed inputs re-render), with no correctness diff, no adjacent-metric regression, and artifacts attached to the CI or review record.
trade_offs: Avoiding render in scroll handler adds state-boundary and memoization discipline; keep the component model readable and remove the optimization if Profiler evidence disappears.
regression_prevention: React Profiler fixture for Avoiding render in scroll handler asserts render_count_per_action; browser INP budget and render-count snapshots fail the pull request on regression.
